definition: A component of Amazon CloudWatch that monitors, stores, and accesses log files from EC2, Lambda, CloudTrail, Route 53, and other sources. Supports retention policies, metric filters that emit CloudWatch metrics, subscription filters to Kinesis/Firehose/Lambda, and log group encryption with KMS.
An interactive query language for searching and analysing log data in CloudWatch Logs. Pay per scanned data; results render in tables or charts that can be saved to dashboards.

Watches a single CloudWatch metric over a specified time period and performs actions (SNS notification, Auto Scaling, EC2 Stop/Reboot/Terminate, Systems Manager OpsCenter incident) when the threshold is breached. Supports composite alarms across multiple metrics.

Configurable scripts (canaries) that monitor endpoints and APIs from outside-in by following customer flows. Detects broken links, slow page load, and outages before customers notice; integrates with CloudWatch alarms.

A capability of AWS Systems Manager that provides secure, hierarchical storage for configuration data and secrets. Plain-text String, StringList, and KMS-encrypted SecureString parameter types; native IAM integration.

A browser-based or CLI shell that gives interactive one-click access to managed EC2 instances and on-prem servers without opening SSH/RDP, bastion hosts, or managing keys. Sessions are logged to CloudWatch Logs / S3 for audit.

Automates the process of patching managed instances with both security and other types of updates. Patch baselines define which patches are approved; maintenance windows schedule when patches are applied.

Lets you create runbooks (Automation documents) to perform common maintenance and deployment tasks across AWS resources. Includes AWS-curated documents and the ability to author custom JSON/YAML automations.

Extends CloudFormation stacks to provision the same template across multiple accounts and Regions in a single operation. Used for organization-wide guardrails, IAM baselines, and multi-account landing-zone deployments.

Detects whether a stack's actual configuration differs from the configuration expected by its CloudFormation template (because someone modified resources outside CloudFormation). Reports per-resource drift status and lets you re-import.

Collects and presents information about your on-premises servers, applications, and network dependencies to plan a migration. Two collectors: agent-based (richer data) or agentless (VMware-only). Data flows into Migration Hub for centralised tracking.

AWS-staffed operations service for enterprises that lets you offload ongoing infrastructure-management tasks. Includes 24/7 incident management, change management, security and patch management, monitoring, and continuity. Two tiers: Accelerate and Advanced.
An offering on top of Enterprise Support that provides proactive monitoring of workloads by AWS engineers. Detects incidents in less than five minutes and engages the customer for rapid mitigation.
